div.head: The Minister in the Irish Free State (Sterling) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 15. Department’s circular December 5, 4 p.m.Not printed. Your instruction December 1, 4 p.m., was carried out promptly with the Foreign Office in the absence of the Minister for External Affairs in London. No action was taken in his absence. I saw him yesterday on his return and he stated that in view of the Soviet’s reply he doubted that Free State support would now serve any useful purpose. He would, however, take it up with the Cabinet next Tuesday.December 10, 1929.
There has been no press comment.
